items.title: Super tunnel aims to capture 95% of millions of tonnes of sewage polluting River Thames
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Environmentalists are set to judge whether a massive engineering feat to install a giant tunnel deep beneath London's River Thames has accomplished its aim to clean up the capital city's polluted waterway. Before work began on the super sewer, there were 60 spills of waste water to the river each year, containing hazardous pathogens like e-coli.


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Staff from Argentina's main pediatric hospital go on strike protesting president's budget veto
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Social unrest grew on Friday with protests after Argentine President Javier Milei vetoed laws that provided funding for public universities, pediatric hospitals, and regional governments. (AP Video shot by Victor R. Caivano)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: Syria's interim president says negotiations with Israel for security deal are ongoing
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Asked if an agreement with Israel will be reached before his expected visit to New York to take part in the UN General Assembly later this month, Syria's interim President Ahmad al-Sharaa said negotiations are still ongoing. He hopes once the deal is reached, Israeli forces will return to where they were before the fall of Assad’s government.


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Number of displaced reaching Deir al-Balah continues to rise as Israel ramps up strikes on Gaza City
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Displaced from Gaza City, Palestinians arrive in the already overcrowded area of Deir al Balah, fleeing once more from the Israeli offensive. But the issue now is that there is not much space left in the area to host the increasing numbers of displaced people. (AP Video by Abdel Kareem Hana)

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Rescue efforts are still underway after seven miners were trapped in an illegal gold mine in southeast Colombia. (AP Video by Juan Diaz)

items.title: Protesters keep up pressure on Philippines government over corruption in flood control projects
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Hundreds of anti-corruption protesters marched in Manila on Saturday morning over allegations of massive corruption in flood control projects that have implicated several congressmen and officials.

items.title: Charlie Kirk's widow gives first statement after conservative activist's killing
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>In a livestreamed speech, Erika Kirk thanked US President Donald Trump, US Vice President JD Vance and supporters who had "shown their love" for her husband. Kirk, the CEO and co-founder of the conservative youth organization Turning Point USA, was a top podcaster, culture warrior and ally of Trump. 


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.title: Nun ousted from a U.S. branch of the Russian Orthodox Church after criticizing invasion of Ukraine
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Before 2022, Sister Vassa Larin was best known as an Orthodox nun who taught thousands on YouTube about saints and holy days.

Then Russia invaded Ukraine and Larin publicly opposed it. Eventually her bishop revoked her status as a nun.

#nun #russianorthodox #ukraine #shorts</article>

items.title: More than 100,000 people pack streets of central London in march organized by far-right activist
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Aerial footage showed demonstrators taking to the streets of central London for a march organized by far-right activist Robinson. Counter-protests organised by groups including 'Stand Up To Racism' were also staged in the capital, drawing significant crowds.

items.title: Candidate for slain Minnesota lawmaker’s seat says he’s working to bring voters together
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Residents in the district of former Minnesota House Speaker Melissa Hortman will choose between Democrat Xp Lee and Republican Ruth Bittner in a special election Tuesday. (AP video: Mark Vancleave)

items.title: Homes damaged in neighborhood in Sanaa, Yemen after Israeli strikes
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Dozens of homes in Sanaa’s Tahrir neighborhood were damaged after Israel's deadly airstrikes this week targeting Iran-backed rebels in Yemen. Residents told The Associated Press they cannot afford any major repairs and that the local authorities are not offering compensation or help with reconstruction.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Nigerian chef Hilda Baci cooks world’s largest pot of jollof rice in Lagos
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Baci, who rose to global fame in 2023 when she set the Guinness World Record for the longest cooking marathon, said her latest effort was about showcasing Nigeria’s food culture. The event is believed to be a fresh chapter in the region’s long-running “jollof wars”—the friendly rivalry among West African nations over who makes the best version of the dish.

(AP video by Dan Ikpoyi)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Lebanese dancer Alexandre Paulikevitch defies threats with sold-out Beirut show
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The Lebanese dancer’s sold-out performance to a cheering crowd at a popular Beirut venue had angered fundamentalist movements ranging from the right-wing Christian Soldiers of God to Sunni Islamists, who say Paulikevitch is “promoting homosexuality,” because he wears dresses and corsets and undulates to classical Arabic music.

(AP video shot by Malak Harb)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Celebrations in Sao Paulo after Bolsonaro jailed for coup attempt, AP explains
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Four of the five Supreme Court justices reviewing the case found Jair Bolsonaro guilty on five counts, in a ruling that will deepen political divisions. The five counts are: attempting a coup after losing the 2022 race to President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva in a plot that prosecutors alleged included plans to kill Lula; participating in an armed criminal organization; attempted violent abolition of the democratic rule of law; damage qualified by violence; and deterioration of listed heritage.

(AP Video shot by Eduardo François; Production by Tatiana Polastri)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: 'It's kind of split': Windsor prepares for Trump's state visit to the UK
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Trump will make an unprecedented second state visit to the UK between Sept. 17 and 19 when he will be hosted by King Charles III and Queen Camilla at Windsor Castle, to the west of the capital. Trump, who is a big supporter of the royal family, particularly of the monarch, will be accompanied by his wife, Melania Trump, during the three-day visit.

(AP Video by Kwiyeon Ha)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>
